$schema_note: Maintenance & operations definition — routines, runbooks, support, and lifecycle management

daily: Automated: DB backup 02:30, OSAS nightly sync 02:00, session pre-generation 03:00, at-risk scan 18:00, token/notification cleanup 04:00
Check uptime monitor + overnight alerts (5 min)
weekly: Review OSAS sync logs for PARTIAL results
Check disk usage and DB size growth
per_semester: Create new academic term + switch is_current
Import/verify schedules and calendar (holidays, suspensions) from OSAS
Archive previous term sessions/records to archive schema
Restore drill from a real backup
Access review: deactivate separated faculty/staff accounts
annual: Rotate secrets (JWT, QR key, OSAS key, SMTP)
Prune 5-year-old archived data per retention policy

runbooks.deploy: Merge to main → CI deploys → verify /healthz + one live scan in staging-mode smoke → announce in maintenance banner if downtime expected
runbooks.rollback: docker compose pull previous tag → up -d → verify /healthz; DB migrations are forward-compatible (destructive changes staged over two releases) so old image runs on new schema
runbooks.osas_outage: Symptoms: student logins failing with OSAS_UNAVAILABLE, sync FAILED alerts. Action: confirm via /integrations/osas/status → notify OSAS office → existing sessions keep working (cached profiles) → when restored, trigger manual syncs → verify sync logs SUCCESS
runbooks.database_restore: Stop api → restore latest dump via restore script → prisma migrate status check → start api → verify healthz + spot-check today's attendance
runbooks.stuck_open_sessions: If autoclose job fails: list sessions status=OPEN past cutoff → run close command per session (marks absents) → investigate job logs
runbooks.compromised_account: Revoke all refresh tokens (family) → force password reset (staff) or coordinate with OSAS (student) → review audit logs for actions in window → document incident
runbooks.certificate_failure: certbot renew --force-renewal → nginx reload → verify; alert fires 14 days before expiry so this should be preemptive

severities.SEV1: Attendance capture down during class hours — respond immediately, workaround: faculty manual attendance; postmortem required
severities.SEV2: Degraded (slow scans, realtime down w/ polling fallback, OSAS outage) — respond same day
severities.SEV3: Non-blocking bug or cosmetic issue — next release

process: Communicate via maintenance banner + admin notification
Postmortem note in ops log for SEV1/SEV2
incident_response.manual_fallback: Faculty manual attendance entry is the universal fallback — the system never blocks a class from recording attendance

support_model.tier_1: Department Admin handles user questions (login issues, correction workflow, report requests)
support_model.tier_2: System maintainer handles bugs, data fixes (always via audited admin actions, never direct SQL), and OSAS coordination

common_requests.student_cannot_login: Check OSAS status (active?) → check lockout → check OSAS health endpoint
common_requests.scan_rejected: Check rejection reason in attendance logs (expired window / not enrolled / closed session / duplicate) — each has a specific user-facing message
common_requests.wrong_attendance_status: Direct student to correction request flow; staff never edits without the workflow
common_requests.faculty_new_class: Dept Admin assigns offering; faculty access follows automatically

support_model.feedback_loop: In-app feedback link → triaged monthly into the module backlog

updates_and_dependencies.cadence: Security patches: within a week (Dependabot PRs auto-tested by CI). Minor updates: monthly batch. Major upgrades (Node LTS, Postgres, React): per-semester planned task on staging first
updates_and_dependencies.database_migrations: Prisma migrations only; destructive migrations require a fresh backup + staging verification; never edit applied migrations
updates_and_dependencies.definition_sync_rule: Any behavior change must update the definition files (SystemDefinition*.json, definition_data/**) in the same PR — definitions are the contract

data_administration.retention: Attendance + audit: 5 academic years live/archive, then purge; notifications: 90 days after read; logs: 12 months
data_administration.archival: Term-end job moves closed sessions/records to archive schema; reports can query archives on demand
data_administration.data_fixes: Only through audited admin endpoints — direct DB writes prohibited in production
data_administration.exports_for_registrar: Semester attendance summaries exported and handed off per institutional records procedure

documentation_upkeep: README + definition_data kept current (enforced in PR review checklist)
Ops log (deployments, incidents, drills) maintained per semester
.env.example updated whenever a config key is added
Admin/faculty user guides refreshed each semester before classes start
